summary: Brainstorm research-grounded method development to make M3 forgetting-bypass generalize to CIFAR (fix phase-context drift/non-separability)

proposals.name: PHLOX: Phase-Locked Library of Oscillator-eXemplar attractors (grow-and-freeze Kuramoto bias-anchors with an energy-MARGIN selector trained against its own falsifiers)
proposals.core_mechanism: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J + the differentiable learned pool P + hypernet g on TASK 0 only, then HARD-FREEZE that shared substrate. Each new task t gets a parameter-disjoint slot: a low-rank Kuramoto bias-anchor b_t (a rank-r U_tV_t^T field injected at the existing kupdate conditioning point, c -> c + b_t) plus a generator column g_t, both frozen once task t ends. b_t reshapes the energy landscape so task-t inputs settle into a DISTINCT deep fixed point (Menara recipe: train b_t so intra-task synchrony >> cross-anchor synchrony). Label-free recall runs the L1 recurrence once per stored anchor and picks the basin by an ENERGY-MARGIN selector e_j(x) = pooled converged AKOrN energy es=-sim under b_j (the value KLayer already returns), trained as a margin (right anchor lowest by gap delta) rather than as a bare argmin. The won slot {b_{j*}, g_{j*}} produces context + head; trunk stays frozen-random.
proposals.literature_grounding: Builds on FOUR cited results plus the repo's own falsification log. (1) Menara+2018 cluster-sync stability (intra>>inter coupling => stable distinct clusters) is operationalized as the energy-margin loss L_margin that makes within-anchor convergence deeper than cross-anchor by a fixed gap. (2) Essex+2026 / Universal-Hopfield (new point-attractor basins = memory; sharper separation = capacity) is operationalized as the Kuramoto energy es=-sim (already computed in KLayer.project) being the basin-depth scalar the selector reads, with grow-and-freeze creating one new basin per task and never destroying old ones. (3) CoRe-2026 low-rank interventions + grow/freeze = the per-task rank-r dJ_t/anchor with structural parameter isolation (the only C3 mechanism that survived the repo's own critique). (4) The repo's PALR M0 artifact (co-trained learned pool lifts the shapes task-channel 0.14->0.39) is the load-bearing reason I KEEP the differentiable learned pool P; the step4b null (live contrastive on phase) and step4a null (frozen-J argmin selector is coupling-agnostic) are why I REPLACE bare argmin with a co-trained energy-margin and explicitly omit any contrastive separation term.
proposals.how_it_fixes_cifar_drift: The diagnosed CIFAR failure is two-fold (overlap + drift); PHLOX attacks each at the substrate, not by regularization hope. SEPARABILITY: on CIFAR the settled phase barely separates tasks (step5 task_lift 0.002-0.21, R6~R6s) because all tasks share one drifting basin landscape. b_t injects a per-task symmetry-breaking bias that PULLS each task's inputs to its OWN deep fixed point; the energy gap between anchors (Menara intra>>inter, enforced by L_margin) is what makes the converged contexts mutually far even on natural images, instead of an incidental by-product of classification. STABILITY/non-drift: the shared coupling J and pool P (the ONLY parameters whose change would move old basins) are frozen after task 0, and old b_j/g_j are requires_grad_(False) -- task B's optimizer physically cannot move task A's basin, so the c->theta map g_A built for A is preserved. This converts the catastrophic forgetting=0.6 into structural retention; the only residual error surface is selector mis-routing, which is measured directly and damped by tiny raw replay.
proposals.what_trains_vs_frozen: FROZEN ALWAYS (C1): the random task-agnostic trunk feeding the generated head (phase->theta is the only adaptive path); AKOrN omega/natural-frequency terms. SHARED-then-FROZEN after task 0: coupling J (KLayer.connectivity), the differentiable learned pool P, c_norm/gamma -- co-trained end-to-end during task 0 so the binding substrate is learned (R6 arm; in R6s these are frozen-random, the C4 control). TRAINED per task t then frozen: low-rank anchor b_t=U_tV_t^T (few hundred params), generator column g_t, and the selector's per-anchor margin parameters. The selector head and replay are CCC-legal re-feeding. One pre-set value per hyperparameter (r, K, d_ctx, delta-margin, lambda_margin, lambda_replay) declared before any GPU run.
proposals.label_free_mechanism: At test no task id. For input x: compute trunk(x) once; run the cheap L1 Kuramoto recurrence once per stored anchor b_1..b_T (T short rollouts) with FIXED oscillator init (h3_seeded(0) so c(x) is stable batch-to-batch -- the determinism bug the prior critique flagged); for each anchor read the pooled converged energy e_j(x)=mean(es=-sim) under b_j. Select j* = argmin_j e_j (deepest basin = best synchronized = lowest energy), trained so the right anchor wins by a margin delta. Route the won slot {b_{j*}, g_{j*}} to produce context and head. Task supervision enters ONLY at train time (which anchor to deepen) = legitimate class-IL; no label at inference.
proposals.anti_forgetting: C3 by STRUCTURAL parameter-isolation, not regularization (the repo proved EWC-in-phase is the wrong side of the CCC bound). Three stacked guarantees: (1) old b_j and g_j set requires_grad_(False) at task boundary -> task t+1's optimizer cannot move task t's anchor or head; (2) the shared J/P/c_norm that define ALL basins are frozen after task 0 -> the substrate that would couple basins never drifts (this is the deep co-training hazard the CIFAR run hit); (3) settling input x under frozen b_a always reproduces task a's basin around its fixed point regardless of how many later tasks were added. Forgetting of dynamics+heads is structurally ~0; the only residual is selector mis-routing, which raw replay (CCC-legal) protects.
proposals.cheapest_milestone: GO/NO-GO in ~1-2 GPU-hr, 1-2 seeds, on a 2-TASK CIFAR-10 slice (not shapes -- shapes already works, so test where it broke). Build only: differentiable energy readout (pooled es under an injected anchor) + ONE grown anchor b_2 over a frozen task-0 substrate. Measure the SELECTOR energy-margin accuracy: for held-out task-0 vs task-1 CIFAR inputs, does argmin_j e_j(x) over {b_0,b_1} pick the right basin? GO requires THREE things jointly (run the falsifiers in the same slice): selector-acc >= 0.75 (chance 0.5) AND R6 beats R6s (frozen-random J) by a clear paired margin AND R6 beats the trivial centroid-L2-distance baseline (anchor=stored task-centroid, energy=L2 to centroid). If R6 ties R6s or ties the centroid baseline, the energy basin is coupling-agnostic/decorative -> NO-GO, accept the M3-CIFAR negative.
proposals.main_risk: Selector collapse on CIFAR: the energy gaps between anchors may be too small on natural images (cross-anchor synchrony ~ within-anchor), so argmin_j e_j mis-routes and accuracy is capped by selector error toward the ~0.25 collapse -- the exact failure the energy-argmin critique predicted (the quantity was coupling-agnostic in step4a). Secondary: R6 ties R6s (PALR M0 already showed co-training washes out the learned-coupling advantage on the channel), which would make any win progressive-net plumbing, not synchrony -> a scientific NULL on the thesis even if accuracy rises. Both are caught by the cheapest-milestone falsifiers before any expensive run.
proposals.novelty: Genuinely new vs prior work: (1) vs the repo's PALR -- PALR routed via a learned-pool context classifier and was silent on basin depth; PHLOX routes via the AKOrN's OWN converged energy (es=-sim) shaped by a Menara intra>>inter MARGIN loss, making basin separation a trained, falsifiable physical quantity rather than an incidental readout. (2) vs the repo's naive PALM -- PALM used a bare energy-argmin shown to rest on a coupling-agnostic quantity; PHLOX trains the energy as a margin AND bakes the two falsifying controls (R6s + centroid baseline) into the GO gate, so it cannot ship a decorative selector. (3) vs Menara/Essex/Hopfield theory -- those describe static basins; PHLOX is the first to GROW Kuramoto cluster-attractor basins online with parameter-disjoint frozen anchors over a frozen learned substrate, giving Hopfield-style non-destructive memory under fully-online class-IL with label-free energy recall. (4) vs the failed CIFAR co-train -- it froze nothing, so basins drifted; PHLOX's freeze-after-task-0 + grow is the structural fix for the diagnosed drift.

proposals.name: PhaseClust-CoRe: Menara-stable phase-cluster contexts with low-rank representation-space drift control
proposals.core_mechanism: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J and a small differentiable learned attention-pool P over the L1 phase field (PALR's validated pool) into a context c, but add three things the diagnosis demands. (1) SEPARABILITY: drive each task into its own STABLE Kuramoto phase-cluster using Menara+2018's stability recipe made into a loss — give task t a learnable per-group natural-frequency offset Omega_t via AKOrN's existing OmegaLayer (set global_omg=False so Omega is per-group/learnable) and an intra>inter cluster-coupling margin, so distinct tasks settle to distinct, asymptotically-stable phase clusters. The pooled context c is then projected to a sparse block-code (SBC/Frady-Kleyko) so task clusters land in near-orthogonal sub-blocks. (2) DRIFT CONTROL (the seed): freeze the shared {J, P, Omega-base} after task 0; for t>=1 learn ONLY a rank-r intervention dC_t = U_t V_t^T applied in the CONTEXT representation space (CoRe-style) plus a per-task generator column g_t, with all dC_{<t}, g_{<t} frozen. (3) ANCHORING: distill old-task context CENTROIDS via Centroid-Distance-Distillation — store only per-task centroid + radius, penalize live c of replayed exemplars from drifting off their stored centroid (||c_live - mu_t||) and penalize centroid-to-centroid distance change. The generated head theta=g(c) on a frozen-random trunk stays the only task-adaptive path.
proposals.literature_grounding: Builds directly on five cited results. CoRe (Luo+2026, arXiv:2603.11201) — the spine: task-specific intervention in a LOW-RANK subspace of the hidden REPRESENTATION (here the phase-context c, not weights) with an explicit stability objective; I apply its representation-space-not-weight-space move to c. Centroid Distance Distillation (Liu+2023, arXiv:2303.02954) — store only old-task centroid DISTANCES to counter rehearsal-induced 'continual domain drift in feature space'; I distill phase-context centroids so old contexts can't drift. Menara+2018 (arXiv:1806.06083) — the genuinely new bridge: cluster synchronization is asymptotically STABLE iff intra-cluster coupling >> inter-cluster OR distinct natural frequencies; I turn this exact theorem into the per-task Omega_t + intra/inter coupling-margin objective, so the SAME condition that makes a task's phase-cluster stable to perturbation is what makes its basin resist being dragged when J trains on later tasks (C3). Frady/Kleyko/Sommer sparse block-codes + Hersche/Rahimi Factorizers-for-SBC (arXiv:2303.13957, shown replacing a CNN's FCL on CIFAR-100) — project c into near-orthogonal block-codes so per-task low-rank interventions are non-colliding. E2-LoRA (arXiv:2605.27482) confirms output-feature drift is inherently low-rank, justifying rank-r dC_t.
proposals.how_it_fixes_cifar_drift: The measured CIFAR failure is TWO compounding problems, and the seed alone fixes only one. Joint upper-bound (no CL, no drift) already caps at acc_real 0.357 with phase task_lift only 0.16 (R6) vs 0.12 (R6s), seed-unstable 0.008-0.21 (step5_cifar n=20) — i.e. CIFAR contexts are NON-SEPARABLE before any drift. step10 then adds drift: forgetting 0.6, R6 ties R6s. SEPARABILITY fix: the Menara per-task Omega_t actively rotates each task's phase-cluster to a distinct frequency band and the intra>inter coupling margin compacts each cluster — this is the lever step4b's contrastive loss lacked (it shaped pooled phase with no dynamical-stability anchor, so it washed out); the SBC projection then makes the clusters near-orthogonal so a 100-way head can read them. STABILITY/drift fix: freeze {J,P,Omega-base} after task0 and confine new learning to rank-r dC_t in context space (no shared substrate to overwrite old basins), with centroid-distance distillation pinning old contexts in place. Crucially the stability is now structural+dynamical (frozen substrate + Menara-stable basins + parameter-disjoint dC_t), not a soft penalty on a moving target.
proposals.what_trains_vs_frozen: FROZEN ALWAYS (C1): the random task-agnostic trunk (features f); generated head theta=g(c) is the only adaptive route to logits. TRAINED ON TASK 0 ONLY, then hard-frozen: shared coupling J, the learned attention-pool P, the base OmegaLayer, the SBC projection matrix, and the shared generator body. TRAINED PER TASK t>=1 (everything else frozen): a fresh per-task natural-frequency offset Omega_t (tiny, per-group), a rank-r context-space intervention dC_t=U_t V_t^T (r=4), and a fresh generator column g_t. Anti-drift state stored (not trained): per-task context centroid mu_t + radius for centroid-distance distillation, plus a small raw-exemplar replay buffer (~300, CCC-legal re-feeding). R6 vs R6s ablation: identical pipeline, R6s uses frozen-random J — isolates whether LEARNED synchrony (not just the pool/low-rank/Omega plumbing) carries the win.
proposals.label_free_mechanism: At test no task id is given. An input is run through the frozen J+P+SBC pipeline to produce its live context c. Because each task was trained to a distinct, asymptotically-stable Menara phase-cluster occupying a near-orthogonal SBC sub-block, c self-organizes near exactly one stored centroid mu_t. A label-free router picks the task as t* = argmin_t ||c - mu_t|| (nearest-centroid in the near-orthogonal code — the SBC factorizer/Universal-Hopfield separation step), selecting which frozen dC_t and column g_t to inject. This routes on the CO-TRAINED live context (not the dead frozen-J descriptor that made step4a's router fail R6=R6s), and the Menara distinct-frequency design is what makes nearest-centroid actually separable on CIFAR. Train-time task labels are used only to assign clusters/centroids (legal class-IL); inference consumes input alone.
proposals.anti_forgetting: Three independent guards, none of which lets an online update overwrite an old task's phase region (C3). (1) Structural: after task 0 the shared oscillator substrate {J, P, Omega-base} is hard-frozen, so later tasks cannot re-sculpt the dynamics that define an old basin — the classic 'B drags A out of its basin via J' route is removed. (2) Dynamical (Menara): each task's cluster is built to satisfy the intra>>inter-coupling / distinct-Omega_t stability condition, so even residual perturbation of an old basin is asymptotically self-correcting — old contexts return to their cluster. (3) Parameter-disjoint + distillation: per-task dC_t, g_t are frozen once their task ends (task B's optimizer literally cannot touch A's tensors), and centroid-distance distillation on replayed exemplars actively penalizes any drift of old contexts off their stored centroids.
proposals.cheapest_milestone: M0 GATE (1-2 GPU-hr, 1-2 seeds, CIFAR-10 joint upper-bound, NO CL/grow/freeze yet): add per-task Omega_t + the Menara intra/inter coupling-margin loss + SBC projection to the existing differentiable PALR pool, co-train {J,P,Omega,SBC,g} jointly, and measure (a) within-task-shuffle TASK-level lift and (b) nearest-centroid label-free task-routing accuracy on held-out CIFAR test inputs (chance 0.20 for 5 tasks). GO if R6 task-routing >= 0.45 AND R6 beats R6s by a clear paired margin (>= +0.07) AND const-context stays ~chance. This is decisive and cheap: it isolates whether the Menara+SBC separability mechanism makes CIFAR phase-contexts separable at all (the step5 ceiling) BEFORE spending any GPU on the grow/freeze/distill CL machinery. If R6 ties R6s or routing ~ chance, the separability lever is dead on natural images and we accept the CIFAR negative.
proposals.main_risk: The frozen-random 64-d trunk may cap CIFAR accuracy regardless of how separable the context is — even a perfect oracle one-hot context only reached ~0.34-0.52 in step5, because the trunk cannot represent CIFAR. If so, no context channel (however separable/stable) can route to good accuracy, and a clean R6>R6s gap would still sit at a low absolute ceiling. Mitigation that stays C1-legal: widen the FROZEN-RANDOM trunk (still task-agnostic, never trained — unbypassability is about task-adaptivity, not capacity) and first run the cited PSA-critique check (does a richer frozen-random trunk lift the oracle ceiling to ~0.8?); only chase the phase channel if the oracle headroom exists. Secondary risk (C4): the Menara/SBC/low-rank plumbing could let R6s match R6 — pre-registered kill if PALR-R6s ties PALR-R6 at n>=5 exact paired test.
proposals.novelty: Genuinely new vs the cited work: (1) Using Menara+2018's Kuramoto CLUSTER-SYNCHRONIZATION STABILITY THEOREM as a continual-learning training objective AND freeze rule — per-task learnable natural-frequencies Omega_t + an intra>inter coupling margin — so the same dynamical condition simultaneously delivers task SEPARABILITY and anti-DRIFT stability. No prior CL work (CoRe, Centroid-Distillation, the LoRA-CL family) uses oscillator cluster-sync stability; no AKOrN work uses it for CL. (2) Applying CoRe's low-rank representation-space intervention and Centroid-Distance-Distillation to an OSCILLATOR PHASE-CONTEXT (they target weight/feature space of static nets) and routing label-free by nearest-centroid in a sparse-block-code phase space. (3) The synthesis closes the exact diagnosed gap the prior PALR campaign left open: PALR's learned pool maxed the SHAPES channel but tied R6=R6s and was never made to separate CIFAR; PhaseClust-CoRe adds the missing separability mechanism that is, by construction, coupling-specific (frozen-random R6s cannot satisfy a learned-frequency cluster-stability condition), giving a principled reason R6 should beat R6s on natural images.

proposals.name: PhaseVSA-WTA: Sparse Block-Code Quantization of the Phase Context (label-free, grow-and-freeze)
proposals.core_mechanism: Insert a sparse block-code quantizer Q between the (live, differentiable) phase descriptor and the hypernet g. Take the L1 phase field, run torch_group_dirs + LearnedPhasePool (already built in /private/tmp/palr_pool.py) to get a continuous context u in R^d. Then split u into M blocks; in each block, score u against a frozen-random per-block codebook of L codevectors (random near-orthogonal anchors) and apply a hard winner-take-all (straight-through estimator for grads): the block emits a one-hot over L. The concatenation is an M-of-(M*L) SPARSE BLOCK CODE c_vsa that the hypernet conditions on (g: c_vsa -> theta on the FROZEN-RANDOM trunk, the only adaptive path). The codebook is frozen-random and fixed for all tasks (so codes are near-orthogonal by construction, Frady/Kleyko/Sommer 2106.05268, capacity bounds Clarkson 2301.10352); only LearnedPhasePool + g train. This replaces the continuous, drifting, overlapping descriptor with a discrete near-orthogonal code — re-creating, label-free, the discreteness that made the oracle one-hot context retain 0.524/forgetting 0.023 through the identical g+trunk.
proposals.literature_grounding: Builds on three cited results plus the repo's own oracle. (1) Hersche/Rahimi "Factorizers for Distributed Sparse Block Codes" 2303.13957 (arxiv.org/abs/2303.13957) is the direct precedent: it replaces a CNN's FC classification layer with a sparse-block-code interface that decodes NOISY product vectors emitted by conv features, validated on CIFAR-100/ImageNet — exactly the "noisy descriptor -> clean discrete code" step on natural images that my Q performs. (2) Frady/Kleyko/Sommer VSA framework 2106.05268 / 37868615 supplies block-local codes whose quasi-orthogonality is what gives minimal cross-task interference; Clarkson 2301.10352 gives the formal capacity/near-orthogonality bounds (random codes in dimension d give interference ~1/sqrt(d), so M*L blocks separate tasks with high probability). (3) Menara 1806.06083 (cluster-sync stable iff intra >> inter coupling) motivates the grow-and-freeze coupling delta so each task's phase settles into its own basin. The load-bearing internal anchor is the repo's OWN oracle (step4c: one-hot context -> final 0.524/forget 0.023 vs continuous phase 0.131/0.536 through the SAME generator) which proves a discrete separable code closes the entire gap.
proposals.how_it_fixes_cifar_drift: It attacks the measured failure (continuous phase contexts overlap + drift) at its root by changing the context FORMAT, not the dynamics (which step4b proved un-shapeable) or the readout alone (which only helped the non-synchrony arm). SEPARABILITY: hard WTA over frozen-random codebooks makes any two contexts either identical (same winners) or near-orthogonal (Clarkson bound), so distinct tasks land on distinct codewords with overwhelming probability even from noisy CIFAR descriptors — directly converting the unstable continuous u into the discrete regime where the oracle already retains 0.52. STABILITY/no-drift: the code is piecewise-constant — small drift in u that does not cross a Voronoi boundary leaves c_vsa EXACTLY unchanged, so g's c_vsa->theta map for an old task is not perturbed by continued training (the continuous descriptor had no such dead-zone, hence final 0.131). The high CIFAR seed-variance of task_lift (R6 std 0.056) is precisely boundary-noise that quantization absorbs. A margin/commitment loss (push u toward its winning codevector while the task is current) sharpens basins so test inputs land cleanly inside, not on boundaries.
proposals.what_trains_vs_frozen: FROZEN ALWAYS: (a) the random task-agnostic conv trunk feeding the generated head (C1 — generated theta is the only adaptive route to logits); (b) the per-block VSA codebooks (random near-orthogonal anchors, fixed for all tasks — this is what makes codes orthogonal-by-construction and is NOT data-adaptive). TRAINED: (a) LearnedPhasePool P (the differentiable attention-pool, <5k params); (b) the AKOrN coupling J (R6 arm) co-trained end-to-end so phase aligns with code basins; (c) hypernet g. ANTI-FORGETTING STRUCTURE (grow-and-freeze): per task t, a fresh low-rank coupling delta dJ_t = U_t V_t^T (rank ~4) added at the KLayer conditional-stimulus injection and a fresh generator column block g_t; previously allocated dJ and g columns are frozen, so learning task B cannot rewrite task A's slow params. Small raw replay (~300, already in loop) only stabilizes P and the current g_t.
proposals.label_free_mechanism: At test there is no task id and none is needed: the input image drives the frozen trunk + co-trained AKOrN, P pools the L1 phase to u, and Q's hard WTA over the FIXED codebooks deterministically selects winners block-by-block from u alone — c_vsa self-organizes from the input. Because the codebook is shared and frozen, an input from task t whose phase settled in task t's basin produces task t's codeword, which g already maps to task t's head. Routing to the grown slow-params is implicit: the codeword IS the address. No argmax-over-task-energy selector is needed (the repo showed that frozen-J selector is dead, step4a), because the codebook lookup, not a learned task classifier, does the selection. C2 verified by the existing label-free-routed vs oracle-routed gap and the const-context~chance / wrong-context-dP5 invariants.
proposals.anti_forgetting: Three layers satisfy C3 (the oscillator/coupling must not overwrite old phase regions). (1) PIECEWISE-CONSTANT CODE: WTA quantization gives a finite-width dead-zone — gradient updates to J/P during task B that move an old task's u within its Voronoi cell produce ZERO change to its c_vsa, hence zero change to the theta g generates for it (the structural cause the continuous descriptor lacked). (2) GROW-AND-FREEZE: per-task dJ_t and g_t are parameter-disjoint and frozen once allocated, so old task-phase regions are protected by construction, not by a soft penalty (Menara-style fresh basins). (3) The frozen codebook itself never updates, so the address space for old codewords is permanent. A replayed prototype codeword per old task keeps g's map for that codeword live even if raw exemplars age out.
proposals.cheapest_milestone: M0 (1-2 GPU-hr, CIFAR-10, JOINT no-CL, no grow/freeze): drop Q (frozen-random codebook + straight-through WTA) between the existing LearnedPhasePool and g in run_joint; unit-test that grad flows through STE to P and J and that c_vsa is exactly invariant to small u perturbations (the dead-zone). Then measure within-task-shuffle TASK-level lift on SplitCIFAR-10 vs the continuous-context baseline (R6 task_lift 0.160, std 0.056) AT MATCHED ctx_dim. GO if quantized R6 task_lift >= +0.06 over continuous AND seed-std drops (the stability prediction) AND const-ctx stays ~chance. This is decisive and cheap: it directly tests whether discretizing the context recovers oracle-like separability on natural images before any CL/grow machinery is built. NO-GO (ties continuous, or no variance drop) kills the format hypothesis cleanly.
proposals.main_risk: The STE hard-WTA may make the assignment of CIFAR inputs to codewords COLLISION-prone: if many tasks' noisy phase descriptors fall in the same block-winner pattern, codes overlap and the oracle gap reopens (separability fails despite discreteness). Equivalently, P+J may fail to push distinct tasks across codebook boundaries (the dual of the dead step4b contrastive result — if dynamics genuinely cannot separate, quantization just snaps overlapping clouds to the SAME codeword). The M0 within-shuffle test is designed to catch exactly this: if quantized task_lift ties continuous, the phase channel's modest task capacity is a true architectural ceiling on CIFAR, not a format artifact, and the project's pre-registered M3-negative stands. Secondary risk: codebook size M,L is a hyperparameter — must be pre-set (one value) to avoid p-hacking, per the locked project standard.
proposals.novelty: Prior repo attempts targeted dynamics (contrastive separation — falsified, step4b) or readout (learned attention-pool — lifted R6s, not R6>R6s). NONE quantized the context. Versus cited VSA work: Hersche 2303.13957 uses sparse block codes to replace a CNN's OUTPUT classifier (supervised, with labels); here the block code is the LABEL-FREE CONTEXT ADDRESS for a hypernetwork on a frozen trunk in online CL — a different role (context channel, not classifier) and a different problem (continual non-drift). Versus the project's oracle: the oracle injects the discrete code from the true task id; PhaseVSA-WTA SELF-GENERATES an oracle-like discrete code from phase with no labels at test. The genuinely new claim: a frozen-random sparse-block quantizer turns an oscillator's noisy, drifting phase descriptor into a near-orthogonal, dead-zone-stable context that bypasses forgetting — combining VSA quasi-orthogonality, STE quantization, and grow-and-freeze into the first label-free phase-as-discrete-context channel.

proposals.name: CPA-Lock: Context-Prototype Anchored experts with frozen-substrate synchrony locking (label-free routing in the CO-TRAINED context space, not raw phase)
proposals.core_mechanism: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J, a small differentiable context map P (phase field xs[1][-1] -> context c), and the hypernet g ONLY on task 0, then HARD-FREEZE {J, P, g_shared} as the permanent substrate (this is the M2/STEP-6 setting where the co-trained context already separates tasks ~0.49 vs oracle 0.52). For each task t>=1, grow a small per-task EXPERT = a low-rank generator column g_t plus a per-task bias-anchor b_t injected at the c_norm point of LadderCore.forward (ladder.py:109) that deepens task-t's Kuramoto basin; freeze all earlier experts. Maintain a frozen per-task PROTOTYPE p_t = the EMA centroid of co-trained contexts c on task-t data (computed in the FROZEN context space, NOT raw phase). At test, with no task id, route label-free by softmax(-||c(x)-p_j||^2 / tau) over frozen prototypes to mix experts {b_j, g_j}; classify with sum_j w_j g_j(c) applied to the frozen-random trunk. Anti-forgetting is purely structural: old experts and old prototypes have requires_grad_(False), and the shared substrate that defines all basins is frozen after task 0, so learning task t cannot move task <t's context region, prototype, or head.
proposals.literature_grounding: Builds DIRECTLY on the project's own artifacts: STEP-6 CIFAR joint result (co-trained phase->context->g lifts the task channel 0.24->0.49, near oracle 0.52) proves separable structure EXISTS once context is co-trained -- so the substrate is frozen at exactly that operating point. STEP-9 (n=12, p=0.0005) proves bounded oscillator phase dynamics + replay resist online drift where a plain CNN collapses -- I keep the oscillator + small raw replay (CCC-legal re-feeding) only to stabilize the substrate during task-0 co-training. From the cited literature: Menara+2018 (Kuramoto cluster sync is STABLE iff intra-cluster coupling >> inter-cluster) is the recipe for the per-task bias-anchors b_t -- they raise intra-task coupling so each task's inputs settle into a distinct, stable phase cluster. Essex+2026/Universal-Hopfield (sharper similarity-separation -> capacity; memory = new disjoint basins) motivates grown per-task basins over a frozen landscape rather than reshaping shared J. Probing-Representation-Forgetting (the seed) survives ONLY as frozen class/task PROTOTYPES used as anchors; Centroid-Distance-Distillation motivates storing context CENTROIDS to stop drift. Anti-drift via structural isolation (von Oswald hypernet + progressive/expandable nets) replaces EWC-in-phase, which the CCC no-replay bound (M1 baselines: EWC sits at the no-replay ceiling) and the campaign critique both rule out.
proposals.how_it_fixes_cifar_drift: The CIFAR failure (STEP-10) is two coupled problems: contexts (a) overlap and (b) drift online. CPA-Lock attacks both at their root. SEPARABILITY: instead of routing in the raw frozen-J phase channel (step4a proved that is R6==R6s, only +0.08 over floor -- non-separable), routing happens in the CO-TRAINED context space where the joint CIFAR number is already ~0.49 (near oracle); the per-task bias-anchors b_t further push intra-task coupling up per Menara so each task forms a tighter, more distinct phase cluster -> sharper prototype separation. STABILITY/NO-DRIFT: the substrate {J, P, g_shared} is frozen after task 0, so the map x->c never moves -- a context computed for a task-3 image today is identical next task. Prototypes are frozen snapshots in that fixed space, so nearest-prototype routing targets never move (unlike STEP-10 where g and the context geometry both drifted, overwriting the task->theta map). Per-task experts are parameter-disjoint and frozen, so learning task t's head physically cannot overwrite task <t's head. This converts CIFAR's unstable, overlapping, co-drifting contexts into a fixed, anchored space with structurally isolated readouts -- exactly the two properties the diagnosis says are missing.
proposals.what_trains_vs_frozen: FROZEN ALWAYS (C1): the random task-agnostic trunk -- generated head theta is the only task-adaptive route to logits. TRAINED on task 0 ONLY then HARD-FROZEN: AKOrN coupling J, the differentiable context map P (phase->c), the shared generator g_shared, and the routing temperature tau. TRAINED per task t>=1 (then frozen via requires_grad_(False)): a low-rank generator column g_t (one hidden row in g.fc1 + fan-out, rank ~4) and a per-task bias-anchor b_t at the c_norm injection point. FROZEN snapshots (never trained, EMA-accumulated within their own task then locked): per-task prototypes p_j in the co-trained context space. Tiny raw replay buffer (~20 imgs/task, CCC-legal) is used ONLY to anchor the substrate during task-0 co-training and as a within-task stabilizer for g_t; it is never used to train old experts.
proposals.label_free_mechanism: At test no task id is given. Compute c(x) through the FROZEN substrate (P over the L1 phase field). Route by soft nearest-prototype: w_j = softmax(-||c(x) - p_j||^2 / tau) over the frozen per-task prototypes p_1..p_T. The mixed expert sum_j w_j {b_j, g_j} generates the head. Because prototypes live in the co-trained context space (separable ~0.49 on CIFAR joint, not the dead raw-phase channel), self-organized routing has real signal. During training the router is teacher-forced to the current task's prototype AND annealed toward its own soft w (fixes the train/test knob mismatch the PALACE critique flagged), so the soft-mix operating point is in-distribution at test. C2 is honored: at inference only the input drives c and hence w.
proposals.anti_forgetting: C3 is satisfied structurally, not by regularization (EWC-in-phase was ruled out -- generalization hole + wrong side of the CCC bound). Three stacked guarantees: (1) PARAMETER-DISJOINT EXPERTS: after task t, b_t and g_t get requires_grad_(False); task t+1's optimizer physically cannot move them. (2) FROZEN SHARED SUBSTRATE: J, P, g_shared are frozen after task 0, so the global energy landscape and the x->c map that define ALL basins and prototypes never drift -- the exact thing that broke in STEP-10 (co-drifting J/g) is eliminated. (3) FROZEN PROTOTYPES: routing anchors are locked snapshots in a fixed space, so the label-free selector's targets never move. The only residual failure surface is router mis-routing, which is measured directly (label-free-routed vs oracle-routed final acc) and is well-defined because the context space is fixed.
proposals.cheapest_milestone: FROZEN-SUBSTRATE ROUTING PROBE (1-2 GPU-hr, CIFAR-10, 1 seed, NO grow/freeze, NO new losses yet). Reuse the existing co-trained joint pipeline (STEP-6 reaches ~0.49): after joint co-training task 0, FREEZE {J,P,g}; for each task compute its prototype p_t = mean of co-trained contexts c; then for held-out test inputs measure label-free nearest-prototype routing slot-accuracy in the FROZEN context space (chance 0.20 for 5 tasks). GO if slot-acc >= 0.45 AND R6 > R6s (paired) AND it clearly beats nearest-prototype routing computed in the RAW frozen-J phase space (the step4a-dead channel, expected ~chance+0.08). NO-GO if frozen-context routing ties raw-phase routing or ties chance -> the co-trained separability does not survive freezing into a routable form, and the whole anchoring bet is exhausted on CIFAR (accept negative). This single test isolates the one new load-bearing claim (separability lives in the frozen co-trained context, is routable label-free) before any grow/freeze/CL spend.
proposals.main_risk: The co-trained context's ~0.49 separability may be an ARTIFACT of joint training that collapses once the substrate is frozen and tasks 1..T are presented sequentially: task-0-only co-training may not produce a P/J whose context space generalizes to LATER tasks' class distributions (it was only optimized on task 0), so prototypes for tasks 2..5 could land in overlapping or degenerate regions -> router slot-acc near chance and CIFAR forgetting returns. This is precisely what the cheapest milestone tests first. Secondary risk (C4): if R6s (frozen-random coupling) routes as well as R6 in the frozen context space (plausible -- step4a showed routing is coupling-AGNOSTIC), then any gain is the grow/freeze + prototype plumbing, not learned synchrony -> honest NULL on the synchrony thesis even if accuracy rises. Mitigation: pre-registered R6-minus-R6s gap margin as the headline metric, not R6's absolute number.
proposals.novelty: vs the SEED (Probing-Representation-Forgetting / prototype-contrastive): the seed routes prototypes in raw phase and shapes the dynamics with supervised-contrastive -- BOTH falsified on this exact harness (step4a: raw-phase routing R6==R6s near-floor; step4b n=8: contrastive-on-live-phase is a NULL, coupling-agnostic). CPA-Lock drops contrastive entirely and moves prototypes into the FROZEN co-trained context space where separability is empirically real (0.49). vs PALR (the prior campaign winner): PALR bet on a learned attention POOL as the bottleneck -- the artifacts show the learned pool did NOT beat mean-pool and R6 did NOT beat R6s through it (PALR gate FAILED, STEP-6 note). CPA-Lock does not bet on the pool; it bets on FREEZING the co-trained substrate + structural per-task experts gated by frozen prototypes. vs the STEP-9 working method: that co-trained J/g SEQUENTIALLY and they drifted on CIFAR; CPA-Lock freezes the substrate after task 0 and isolates per-task experts, converting a drifting system into an anchored one. The genuinely new claim: the co-trained context separability is preserved and made label-free-routable by freezing it into prototype anchors over disjoint Kuramoto-basin experts (Menara intra>>inter), rather than by shaping dynamics or by a better readout -- the one lever the artifacts leave open.

proposals.name: FROST: Frequency-Routed Oscillator Sub-Tori (ω-banded grow-and-freeze, drift-controlled, coherence-selected)
proposals.core_mechanism: Give each task its own LEARNABLE NATURAL-FREQUENCY BAND ω_t on the L1 AKOrN oscillators (AKOrN already has per-oscillator learnable omega: use_omega=True, learn_omg=True), grown one-per-task and FROZEN after the task ends, on top of a shared coupling J. By Menara 2018, distinct ω between clusters makes cluster-synchronization a STABLE invariant manifold independent of coupling strength — so task t's inputs settle into a distinct rotating sub-torus (a Hopfield-style basin realized in Kuramoto phase) whose pooled phase is the context c fed to the hypernet g→theta on the frozen-random trunk. The context is read by a band-resolved pool (per-ω-band coherence + group-direction summary, not the single S_N-invariant mean-pool), so different tasks land in geometrically distinct, ORTHOGONAL-by-frequency context regions. Concretely: inject a per-band frequency offset into the omg_x term of dxdt = omg_x + project(connectivity(x)+c, x) at L1; grow ω_t + a hypernet column g_t per task and freeze old ones; protect the shared J with a CoRe/null-space drift penalty on its converged-phase output.
proposals.literature_grounding: Menara+2018 (arXiv:1806.06083, the brief's anchor): cluster synchronization is asymptotically stable iff intra-cluster coupling >> inter-cluster OR natural frequencies of distinct clusters differ sufficiently — FROST uses the SECOND, never-exploited clause (ω, not J) as the separability engine, which is exactly why it survives drift. Essex+2026 (arXiv:2508.10765) / Universal Hopfield (Millidge, PMID 36751405, similarity-separation-projection, sharper separation→higher capacity): a fixed ω-band IS a permanent point/limit-cycle attractor basin; freezing ω_t makes basin-destruction-by-new-stimuli (their forgetting mechanism) structurally impossible, and band-coherence is the 'similarity' score for label-free retrieval. CoRe 2026 (arXiv:2603.11201) + LRFR/null-space (arXiv:2312.08740): the shared J is drift-controlled by a low-rank/null-space stability objective on its converged phase output (the explicit anti-drift the seed asked for). Sparse block-codes / orthogonal MBAT (Frady/Kleyko; arXiv:2202.04771): per-task ω-bands are literally block-local codes — near-orthogonal task codes by construction (frequency multiplexing), giving minimal cross-task interference. Builds on the project's own n=20 result that CO-TRAINING (not the learned pool, not learned synchrony alone) lifts the task channel to ~0.49 near oracle 0.52.
proposals.how_it_fixes_cifar_drift: The diagnosed CIFAR failure is two-part: contexts (a) overlap and (b) DRIFT under online learning because a single shared substrate (J/pool/g) keeps moving and re-sculpts old basins. FROST attacks both at the source. SEPARABLE: distinct learnable ω-bands frequency-multiplex the tasks — task t's oscillators rotate in band t, so even on natural images where spatial-binding structure is weak, the contexts are pushed apart in FREQUENCY (an axis orthogonal to image content), and the band-resolved pool exposes that separation to g (oracle-like near-one-hot-by-band contexts) instead of collapsing it via the S_N-invariant mean-pool. DRIFT-FREE: Menara's theorem guarantees that once ω-bands are distinct, the cluster-sync manifold is stable to PERTURBATIONS of coupling — so even as the shared J drifts while learning task t+1, task t's basin (defined by its FROZEN ω_t) does not move. This is the qualitative difference from PhaseLock/PALACE, which tried to hold basins in place by penalizing a still-moving J (a generalization-gap losing battle on CIFAR); FROST makes the basins drift-immune by construction, then uses the CoRe null-space penalty only as a second line to keep J's shared binding usable.
proposals.what_trains_vs_frozen: FROZEN ALWAYS (C1): the random task-agnostic trunk (the nn.Sequential conv→64-d features); the generated head theta(c) is the only task-adaptive path to logits. CO-TRAINED on task 0 then FROZEN: the shared coupling J (KLayer.connectivity) and the band-resolved pool P — learned once so binding exists, then held (with the CoRe null-space penalty allowing only stability-preserving low-rank nudges if kept live as an ablation). GROWN per task t and then HARD-FROZEN (requires_grad_(False) at the boundary): (a) the per-task natural-frequency offset ω_t injected into omg_x at L1 (~a few hundred params: one offset per oscillator-group), (b) a hypernet column g_t (one hidden row in g.fc1 + its fc2 fan-out). TRAINED on the current task only: ω_t, g_t, and a tiny shared band-selector readout, plus a small raw-exemplar replay (~300, already in the loop) fed through the live pipeline. No old ω_j or g_j ever receives a task-t gradient.
proposals.label_free_mechanism: At test no task id is given. For input x, run the cheap L1 recurrence ONCE per frozen band ω_1..ω_T (T forwards through the small recurrent core; trunk computed once) under a FIXED phase init (h3_seeded, so every candidate shares the same init — fixes the determinism bug the campaign flagged). For each band compute its BAND-LOCAL coherence/synchrony score (the project()'s sim scalar, but pooled WITHIN band j, not globally) = how well x synchronized into basin j. Select j* = argmax band-coherence (a Universal-Hopfield similarity-then-projection retrieval), then use the band-j* context with head g_{j*}. This selector is loaded — unlike the dead frozen-J global descriptor (R6==R6s, +0.08 over floor) — precisely BECAUSE ω-banding creates band-specific coherence that only fires for the on-band task; the selector reads a frequency feature the prior global mean-pool averaged away. The band-selector is trained with current-task id (class-IL legal); inference is input-only (C2). A soft softmax(coherence) mixture over heads is the graceful-degradation fallback.
proposals.anti_forgetting: C3 (oscillator self-forgetting) is closed at three stacked levels, the first being a structural guarantee no prior proposal had. (1) FREQUENCY ISOLATION + Menara stability: each task's basin is pinned by its FROZEN ω_t; Menara 2018 proves distinct-frequency clusters form a stable manifold ROBUST to coupling perturbation, so learning task t+1 (which only moves the shared J slightly and writes a new ω_{t+1}) provably cannot pull task t's inputs out of basin t — the basin's existence does not depend on the part that changes. (2) PARAMETER-DISJOINT GROW-AND-FREEZE (PALM's one surviving idea): old ω_j and g_j have requires_grad_(False), so task t's optimizer physically cannot overwrite them (gradient masked, optimizer state reset for frozen tensors). (3) CoRe/null-space drift control on the shared J: the only shared, still-usable substrate is constrained so its updates lie in the null space of past tasks' converged-phase output (LRFR/CoRe), bounding any residual basin drift. Raw replay re-pins g's shared input layer. This is why FROST beats PhaseLock/PALACE on C3: they fought drift with soft penalties on a moving J; FROST removes the drift's leverage over old basins entirely.
proposals.cheapest_milestone: ~1-2 GPU-hr, 1 seed, 2-task slice of SplitCIFAR-10 (the actual failing testbed, not shapes — go straight at the drift). Implement only: (i) the per-band ω_t offset injection into omg_x at L1 + band-local coherence pool (differentiable torch port, unit-tested against numpy group_directions to 1e-4 with grad flow); (ii) train task 0 {J, P, ω_0, g_0}, freeze J/P/ω_0; grow ω_1, g_1 for task 1, freeze ω_0/g_0. Measure TWO pre-registered numbers: (A) BAND-SELECTOR ACCURACY — label-free argmax-coherence over {ω_0, ω_1} on held-out CIFAR task-0 vs task-1 inputs; GO if >= 0.70 (chance 0.50) AND R6 (learned J) beats R6s (random-frozen J) AND beats a centroid-distance baseline (firewall against the grow-and-freeze-bank-alone cheat). (B) RETENTION — after adding ω_1, is task-0 accuracy via frozen {ω_0,g_0} unchanged (delta < 0.03)? NO-GO and accept the negative if band-selector is at chance on CIFAR (ω-banding does not create separable basins on natural images) — kills the whole bet before any 5-task/multi-seed spend.
proposals.main_risk: The label-free band-selector is still the single point of failure: ω-banding is engineered to MAKE band-coherence task-discriminative on CIFAR (unlike the dead global frozen-J descriptor), but this is the untested empirical crux — if natural-image features do not synchronize differentially across learned ω-bands, argmax-coherence mis-routes and accuracy regresses toward the ~0.20 collapse even with perfectly retained heads (forgetting becomes routing error). This is exactly why milestone (A) targets band-selector accuracy on CIFAR FIRST, with the R6-vs-R6s and centroid-baseline firewalls, so the bet is killed cheaply if the frequency-separation premise does not hold on real images. Secondary risk: ω_t becomes a thinly-disguised per-task tag supervised on task membership (a C4 cheat) — guarded by the wrong-context dP5 and within-task-shuffle invariants plus the must-beat-R6s gap on the GAP (not absolute) metric.
proposals.novelty: Genuinely new vs all 5 prior campaign proposals and the PALR synthesis: every one of them tried to separate tasks by shaping the COUPLING J (PhaseLock supcon, PALACE low-rank dJ, PALM gain s_t, PSA slots) or the READOUT pool (PALR) — both empirically falsified on this project (step4b live-phase-shaping NULL; learned-pool ties mean-pool; frozen-J selector R6==R6s). FROST is the first to use the OTHER Menara clause — distinct NATURAL FREQUENCIES (ω) — as the separability and stability engine, exploiting AKOrN's existing-but-untouched learnable omega. The novelty is mechanistic, not just a new combination: ω-band separation is (a) provably drift-resistant under coupling perturbation (Menara), which directly answers the diagnosed CIFAR drift that coupling/readout methods cannot; (b) frequency-multiplexing = orthogonal block-codes (Frady/Kleyko) for free task-code orthogonality; (c) it makes the previously-dead label-free coherence selector loaded by giving it a band-specific signal to read. The hybrid grafts only the surviving pieces of prior work (grow-and-freeze isolation, CoRe drift control) onto this new core, so it beats each single idea: more separable than coupling/readout-shaping (orthogonal-by-frequency), and more stable than any soft-penalty-on-moving-J scheme (Menara-stable + parameter-disjoint).

critiques.will_it_actually_fix_drift: Drift yes, separability no via freeze-and-grow.
critiques.will_it_forget: No, isolation sound; risk relocated to selector mis-routing.
critiques.label_free_ok: Formally yes; energy reads anchor directly, a leak.
critiques.bug_risk: Differentiable energy readout does not exist yet.
critiques.cheapest_kill_test: Readout test on existing R6 R6s backbones.
critiques.single_best_fix:
Route by centroid not energy. FULL CRITIQUE FOLLOWS. VERDICT risky_but_worth_it. (1) FIXES DRIFT, NOT SEPARABILITY. Freeze-and-grow (freeze J, pool P, c_norm after task 0; freeze old anchors b_j and heads g_j) genuinely stops the diagnosed drift, the one true advance. But the label-free selector is argmin over anchors of pooled energy es, which is neg-sim summed, sim is x times y, klayer.py 121-163. The repo already measured this exact quantity coupling-agnostic and near-empty: step4a R6 mlp_cv 0.294 vs R6s 0.297 identical, floor 0.217; step5 CIFAR acc_real R6 0.357 vs R6s 0.349 tie, acc_within R6s at-or-above R6. The L_margin lever is supervised-contrastive-on-phase renamed, and step4b already ran it NULL, task_lift 0.121 vs 0.124, p 0.84. So contexts become stable-but-overlapping, turning forgetting into stable mis-routing at the same 0.2-0.27 ceiling. (2) FORGETTING: isolation is structurally sound (oracle retains 0.524 at forget 0.02 when routing is right), but C3 is RELOCATED into the selector, not removed; mis-routing grows with task count and the 2-task milestone hides it, so test T 5. The per-task shared anchor-gate and margin params are a shared trainable path that can drift and violate isolation and C1; freeze them. (3) LABEL-FREE LEAKS: energy reads the anchor directly since y is connectivity(x) plus c and the anchor enters as c plus b_j, klayer.py 126-150, so a larger-norm bias shifts energy independent of synchronization and the selector can route on bias-norm not basin depth; L_margin uses task-supervised batches so separability is bought with supervision; about 54 percent of context lift is instance-level so a margin loss can route on exemplar identity. Score selector-acc on within-task-shuffle held-out inputs only. (4) BUGS: the differentiable energy readout under an anchor does NOT exist; m2_hypernet _phase_context runs under torch no_grad through NumPy; palr_pool is a diff pool but not a diff rollout and is not imported. A sign or pooling-axis flip makes the selector anti-correlated while L_margin trains the wrong way and runs green; the port must drop no_grad but keep h3_seeded(0); Adam updates frozen params unless removed from the param-group; pooling over group/site can make energy identical across anchors. (5) CHEAPEST KILL: do not build first; run a 30-min readout test on the already-trained R6 and R6s step5 backbones, no training, inject a per-task anchor that can be the stored mean L1-state offset, score argmin selector-acc on within-task-shuffle held-out inputs for R6 vs R6s vs centroid-L2, and KILL if R6 acc below 0.75, or R6 ties R6s, or R6 ties centroid-L2 (which is Centroid-Distance-Distillation). Prior: dies on the last two. (6) SINGLE BEST FIX: keep freeze-and-grow but replace argmin-energy routing with distance-to-stored-prototype or centroid routing and demote energy to an ablation arm; GO bar is whether energy beats centroid, and if not you still ship a clean grow-and-freeze prototype-routing method plus a measured negative. Note the learned pool PHLOX keeps erases the synchrony gap: palr_m0 learned R6 0.398 equals learned R6s 0.395 while mean-pool was R6 0.389 vs R6s 0.331.</parameter>
</invoke>

critiques.proposal_name: PhaseClust-CoRe: Menara-stable phase-cluster contexts with low-rank representation-space drift control
critiques.will_it_actually_fix_drift: Partly addresses drift, but its separability story is contradicted by your own data, and several premises are mis-cited. (1) The DRIFT half is genuinely sound by construction: freeze {J,P,Omega-base} after task0 + parameter-disjoint per-task dC_t/g_t + centroid distillation makes old basins literally un-overwritable. That removes the step10 mechanism (J/g overwritten online). This is the strongest part and is essentially the design campaign's already-proposed PALM family (grown anchors + frozen shared J + energy/centroid label-free selection) restated. (2) The SEPARABILITY half is the make-or-break, and the evidence says it is likely doomed AS STATED. Verified from results/autorun_2026_06_07: step5 CIFAR joint upper-bound gives R6 acc_real=0.357±0.009, task_lift=0.160±0.056 (range 0.008-0.212, i.e. seed-unstable), R6-R6s task_lift Δ=+0.043 with sign-test p=0.115 (NOT significant, 14/20). The proposal's own M0 gate (R6 task_lift>=0.45 AND R6>R6s by >=+0.07 significant) ALREADY FAILS on existing data with no new mechanism. The Menara Omega_t + intra/inter margin must lift task_lift from 0.16 to >=0.45 AND open a >=0.07 R6>R6s gap that does not currently exist — a 3x improvement on a quantity that is currently noise-dominated. (3) Mis-citation that hides the risk: the proposal says 'oracle one-hot only reached ~0.34-0.52 in step5' and treats it as the CIFAR trunk ceiling. There is NO CIFAR oracle run anywhere — step4c oracle (0.524) is on SHAPES. The actual CIFAR const-context (trunk-only) is already 0.159 and within-shuffle 0.318, so real context lifts only 0.198 to a 0.357 ceiling whose true oracle headroom on CIFAR is UNMEASURED. The 'main_risk' (frozen trunk caps CIFAR) is real and untested, and the cited mitigation ('oracle reached 0.8 with richer trunk') is a hypothetical, not a result.
critiques.will_it_forget: Unlikely to self-forget on the OLD tasks — that part is over-engineered with three redundant guards (frozen substrate + parameter-disjoint dC_t/g_t + centroid distillation). Parameter-disjoint freezing alone guarantees task B's optimizer cannot touch task A's tensors, so C3 is structurally satisfied. The real forgetting-equivalent failure is at the ROUTER: nearest-centroid t*=argmin||c-mu_t|| over near-orthogonal SBC blocks. If CIFAR contexts are non-separable (step5 says they are: task_lift 0.16, within-shuffle ~ real), live c for a new-task input will frequently land nearest a WRONG centroid, injecting the wrong dC_t/g_t -> looks exactly like catastrophic forgetting at test even though no weights were overwritten. The method converts weight-drift forgetting into routing-error forgetting; whether that is better depends entirely on whether separability is actually achieved, which the data currently denies. Also: freezing the shared substrate after task0 means task0's J defines all later basins; if task0 is unrepresentative (a real risk on a 5-way CIFAR split) every later task's basin geometry is hostage to it, with no recovery path.
critiques.label_free_ok: Mechanism is C2-legal in principle (router consumes input-derived c only; task labels used at train time for cluster/centroid assignment = class-IL, allowed). BUT two leak/cheat risks. (1) Storing per-task centroid mu_t + radius and routing by nearest-centroid is a soft task-ID lookup; it is legal only if c is computed from the input with NO task-conditioning at test. Bug-prone spot: the current _phase_context recomputes phase per input so that is fine, but if any per-task Omega_t or dC_t must be APPLIED to produce c before routing (the proposal is ambiguous — Omega_t is per-task and feeds the dynamics that produce c), you have a chicken-and-egg leak: you need the task id to pick Omega_t to produce the c you route on. The PALM design solved this with run-under-every-anchor + min-energy; this proposal's router as written ('run through frozen J+P+SBC to produce c, then argmin') seems to route on the SHARED frozen base only, which means the discriminative per-task Omega_t NEVER influences the routed c — so the very mechanism claimed to create separability is bypassed at inference. That is a likely silent correctness bug. (2) Task-decodability from phase was already shown null/fragile at n=17 (Holm) and via nonlinear MLP — nearest-centroid is just another probe, and the prior probes say the task LABEL is not cleanly decodable from phase even on shapes; on CIFAR routing accuracy near chance (0.20 for 5 tasks) is the live outcome.
critiques.bug_risk: Highest-probability bugs, ranked: (1) palr_pool.py / 'validated differentiable PALR pool' DOES NOT EXIST in the repo, nor does any script that produced the palr_m0_*.json files — the current pool (_pool_phase_state in m2_primitives.py) is numpy, non-differentiable, and the harness detaches+moves to CPU before pooling (m2_hypernet.py L74: .detach().float().cpu().numpy()). Co-training J THROUGH the pool requires a new differentiable pool; calling it 'already built+unit-tested' is false against this tree and is the single biggest hidden dependency. (2) Router/Omega chicken-and-egg leak described above (per-task Omega_t needed to make c separable, but routing happens before task is known). (3) The chosen pool actively erases the effect: on shapes, LEARNED-pool R6 task_lift=0.398 vs R6s=0.395 (Δ0.003, ties) — the proposal builds on the one pool that kills the R6>R6s gap C4 requires; only the MEAN pool preserves a gap (R6 0.389 vs R6s 0.331, Δ0.058). (4) Omega_t is not synchrony-specific: ladder.py already has global_omg/learn_omg and R5 = 'Omega ON, synchrony OFF', so a frozen-random R6s can carry the same per-task Omega_t + margin -> R6s can satisfy the 'Menara cluster-stability condition' too, breaking the proposal's claimed principled reason R6 must beat R6s. (5) SBC near-orthogonal projection of a 2n=8-16-dim context into 'near-orthogonal sub-blocks' for 100 (or even 5) tasks is dimensionally implausible — block codes need high D; an 8-16-dim phase context cannot host many near-orthogonal blocks. (6) plainCNN 'necessity' is confounded: staged plainCNN=0.81-0.83 (no collapse) vs online plainCNN=0.11 with learn_acc 0.47 (under-learns) — the collapse is plausibly an optimization artifact, so the 'oscillator resists drift' premise the anti-forgetting story leans on is not clean.
critiques.cheapest_kill_test: Run the proposal's OWN M0 gate on the EXISTING step5 CIFAR joint data first — it is already computed and it FAILS (R6 task_lift=0.16 not 0.45; R6-R6s=+0.043, sign p=0.115 not significant). Before building anything, do the 1-2 GPU-hr falsifier: add ONLY per-task Omega_t + the intra>inter coupling margin to the joint CIFAR upper-bound (no SBC, no CoRe, no freeze) and measure two numbers on held-out CIFAR test: (a) does R6 task_lift rise from 0.16 toward 0.45, and (b) does the R6-R6s gap exceed +0.07 at n>=10 paired with a significant exact test. CRITICAL: also run the SAME Omega_t+margin on R6s (frozen-random coupling). If R6s task_lift rises just as much (very likely, since Omega is an R5 mechanism independent of learned J), the separability lever is NOT coupling-specific and C4 is dead — kill it. This single 2-arm joint run, with no CL/freeze/SBC machinery, decides the whole proposal and costs almost nothing.
critiques.single_best_fix: Stop claiming the separability mechanism is coupling-specific and instead FIRST measure the CIFAR oracle ceiling (it has NEVER been run): one-hot task context through the frozen-random trunk + hypernet on CIFAR, n>=5. If the CIFAR oracle is ~0.3-0.4 (as the const=0.159/real=0.357 numbers suggest it may be, far below the misattributed shapes-0.52), then NO context channel can route to good accuracy and the entire proposal is chasing headroom that does not exist — you must widen the frozen-random trunk first (C1-legal: capacity, not task-adaptivity) until the CIFAR oracle clears ~0.7, THEN test separability. This reorders the milestones so you spend the 1-2 GPU-hr on the question that gates everything (is there any headroom on CIFAR at all?) before building Menara+SBC+CoRe+centroid-distillation on top of an unmeasured, possibly-zero ceiling. Without this, a 'clean R6>R6s gap' (even if you get one) sits at a low absolute ceiling and is not a publishable CIFAR result.

critiques.proposal_name: PhaseVSA-WTA: Sparse Block-Code Quantization of the Phase Context
critiques.will_it_actually_fix_drift: No. It relabels the problem rather than fixing it. The proposal commits a quantization-of-noise fallacy: hard WTA cannot CREATE mutual information I(task; context) that the continuous descriptor u lacks. Frady/Clarkson near-orthogonality only holds for DISTINCT codewords; whether two tasks get distinct codewords depends entirely on whether u separates them BEFORE quantization. The repo's own artifacts say it does not, on exactly the substrate Q reads. From results/autorun_2026_06_07/step4a_readout.json (the L1 phase that LearnedPhasePool pools): task-decode MLP-CV is R6 ~0.29-0.31 vs shuffle-floor ~0.21-0.24 vs chance 0.20 — only ~0.08 above the within-task floor, and R6s decodes IDENTICALLY (~0.297). So the phase clouds for different tasks already overlap badly and are coupling-agnostic. WTA on overlapping clouds does one of two bad things: (a) snaps two tasks to the SAME block-winners (collisions -> oracle gap reopens, the proposal's own named main_risk), or (b) snaps the SAME task's noisy test-time u to DIFFERENT winners across inputs (within-task code instability -> g sees inconsistent addresses -> chance). The proposal's 'dead-zone stability' argument is the same coin: a wide Voronoi cell that absorbs drift is ALSO a cell that absorbs the (tiny, 0.08-above-floor) cross-task signal, collapsing tasks together. You cannot have a dead-zone wide enough to kill drift but narrow enough to keep tasks apart when the task signal is smaller than the noise — which the artifacts show it is. The cited CIFAR number the proposal leans on (step5 R6 task_lift 0.160, std 0.056) is measured on FROZEN/captured phase in the joint, not online; under actual online CL (step10) CIFAR R6 final=0.256 forget=0.672 and R6s=0.267 — R6s is BETTER, synchrony advantage is negative. And critically palr_m0_*.json shows the LearnedPhasePool that Q sits downstream of gives R6 task_lift 0.398 == R6s 0.395 == mean-pool 0.389: the pool is coupling-agnostic too. Q inherits a substrate where the synchrony channel is already dead.
critiques.will_it_forget: The grow-and-freeze + frozen codebook layer genuinely makes the SLOW params non-overwriting (per-task dJ_t, g_t are parameter-disjoint and frozen; codebook never updates). That part is structurally sound and satisfies C3 mechanically. BUT this is borrowed wholesale from the repo's already-chosen PALR design (synthesis.chosen_design: 'purely STRUCTURAL parameter-isolation, grow+freeze'); it is not novel here and it was never the bottleneck. The forgetting it does NOT prevent is the only one that matters: at test, label-free routing depends on c_vsa landing in the right cell. If task B's training pushes the live, co-trained J/P so that task A's inputs now produce a different winner pattern (the codebook is fixed but u is NOT — J and P keep training), then old codewords get MIS-ADDRESSED and g routes A's inputs to B's column. The 'piecewise-constant dead-zone protects old u' argument only holds if J/P updates keep u inside A's old cell — but J/P are explicitly co-trained on B and there is no constraint binding them to A's cells (step4b already showed you cannot shape live phase to separate tasks; there is even less hope of shaping it to STAY in old cells). So forgetting reappears as routing-drift, masked by the claim that the address space is permanent. Permanent addresses with a moving pointer is still catastrophic forgetting.
critiques.label_free_ok: C2 is structurally respected (codebook lookup is deterministic from u, no task-id at test, no learned task classifier — sidesteps the dead step4a selector). But there are two real leak surfaces. (1) INSTANCE-LEAK: the repo's decomposition shows ~54% of context lift is instance-level, and on CIFAR step5 instance_lift (0.039) is comparable to task_lift (0.160 captured / collapses online). A margin/commitment loss that pushes u toward its winning codevector 'while the task is current' can drive winners by per-exemplar identity rather than task identity, inflating train-time code-purity while failing held-out within-task-shuffle — the exact failure the M3-DESIGN-CAMPAIGN critique flagged for router proposals (results show CIFAR seed-0 nearly pure instance leak). (2) The commitment loss is supervised by 'the current task' implicitly (it knows which exemplars are current), so codebook assignment can become a covert per-task supervised partition; that is class-IL-legal at train but means the GO signal can come from training-time label structure, not from test-time self-organization. The kill-test must gate on held-out within-task-shuffle and wrong-context dP5, never train code-purity.
critiques.bug_risk: Highest-probability bugs, in order: (1) STE gradient is a lie through hard WTA — grads flow to P/J as if Q were identity, but the forward winner can flip discontinuously, so the optimizer chases a surrogate that does not match the deployed code; classic STE collapse where all blocks converge to a few dominant codevectors (code death), silently reducing effective M and reopening collisions. Must instrument per-block codeword-usage entropy, not just 'grad is finite' (the proposed unit test only checks grad finiteness + dead-zone invariance, which will PASS while the code silently collapses). (2) The dead-zone unit test ('c_vsa exactly invariant to small u perturbations') is trivially satisfiable and PROVES NOTHING about separability — it passes even if every task maps to one codeword. It is a comfort test that will give a false green. (3) Frozen-random codebook near-orthogonality requires u to be high-dim and roughly isotropic; LearnedPhasePool emits d_ctx=16, and per-block scoring over L codevectors in low-dim blocks (16/M dims each) destroys the 1/sqrt(d) guarantee — Clarkson bounds need large d, not d=16 split M ways. The orthogonality the whole proposal rests on may not hold at this d. (4) Within-task-shuffle eval must shuffle AFTER quantization; if u is shuffled then quantized vs quantized then shuffled, the metric silently measures the wrong thing. (5) Co-training J end-to-end through STE + grow-freeze + replay is a large surface; the joint-vs-online metric confusion (step5 captured 0.160 vs step10 online collapse) shows how easy it is to report a captured number and believe it transfers.
critiques.cheapest_kill_test: Skip building Q entirely — the kill measurement already exists and has already fired. Take the EXISTING step4a L1 phase descriptors (results/autorun_2026_06_07/step4a_readout.json substrate) on CIFAR, run the proposed frozen-random block-codebook + hard WTA on them OFFLINE (no training, ~20 min CPU), and measure two things: (a) cross-task codeword collision rate (fraction of task pairs whose modal codewords share >50% block-winners) and (b) within-task codeword consistency (fraction of a held-out task's inputs that map to that task's modal codeword). PREDICTION FROM ARTIFACTS: since task-decode is only ~0.08 above shuffle floor and coupling-agnostic, collisions will be high and within-task consistency near chance — i.e., WTA snaps overlapping clouds together. If within-task consistency < ~0.5 OR R6 collision-rate == R6s collision-rate, the format hypothesis is dead before any GPU spend. This is strictly cheaper and more decisive than the proposal's M0 (which co-trains, adding STE/optimizer confounds that can manufacture train-time purity via instance leak). The proposal's own main_risk admits this is the crux but defers it to a trainable M0; the offline version removes the confounds.
critiques.single_best_fix: Stop trying to fix the READOUT/FORMAT (both readout — M2 contrastive-readout null — and dynamics — step4b live-phase contrastive null — are already falsified; quantization is a third readout variant and will share their fate). If there is any swing left, it must INCREASE I(task; phase) in the SUBSTRATE before pooling, which the artifacts say is the true ceiling. The only honest lever consistent with C1-C4 and the cited literature is to give the oscillator stable, task-distinct natural-frequency / coupling structure per task BY CONSTRUCTION (Menara 2018: cluster-sync is stable iff intra >> inter coupling or natural freqs differ) AND measure whether that raises task-decode above the 0.08-over-floor coupling-agnostic ceiling and above R6s — i.e. attack separability at the dynamics with a structural (not contrastive-loss) intervention that step4b did not try. If even that does not move task-decode > R6s on CIFAR, accept the oracle-validated M3 negative: the phase channel's task capacity on natural images is an architectural ceiling, and dressing it in VSA codewords only hides chance behind near-orthogonal vectors. Concretely: keep grow-and-freeze (it works), drop Q, and run the offline collision kill-test first — if it fails, write the negative.

critiques.proposal_name: CPA-Lock (Context-Prototype Anchored experts with frozen-substrate synchrony locking)
critiques.will_it_actually_fix_drift: No. The entire bet rests on a number that does not exist on CIFAR. CPA-Lock repeatedly claims the co-trained context space "is already ~0.49 (near oracle 0.52) on CIFAR joint" and routes prototypes there. That 0.49 is the SHAPES construct, not CIFAR. Verified in artifacts: palr_m0_* (shapes) gives acc_within ~0.485 / acc_real ~0.99; that is where 0.49 comes from. On the actual target (results/autorun_2026_06_07/step5_cifar_*.json, n=20) the routable signal is task_lift: R6 mean=0.160 (sd 0.056), R6s mean=0.116 (sd 0.065) — overlapping distributions, gap ~0.044 — and acc_within is R6 0.318 vs R6s 0.329 (R6s HIGHER). instance_lift (0.04) rivals or exceeds task_lift on several seeds (seed0: task 0.008 / instance 0.043 = near-pure instance leak). So on CIFAR the co-trained context is (a) barely above chance for TASK and (b) coupling-agnostic. CPA-Lock proposes to freeze exactly this near-dead, R6==R6s context and route prototypes in it. Freezing a non-separable space does not make it separable; it just locks in the overlap. The proposal also conflates two different quantities: joint-trained 10-way ACCURACY (acc_real ~0.39-0.48 on CIFAR) with context TASK-SEPARABILITY (task_lift ~0.16). Routing depends on the latter, which is ~chance+0.04 and coupling-agnostic. The "Menara intra>>inter bias-anchors will tighten clusters" mechanism is the same family as step4b's live-phase separation objective, already a confirmed NULL (step4b_lam0 vs lam1, n=8: task_lift 0.137->flat, task_decode_cv 0.43->0.43, p~0.84). So both legs — separable-frozen-context AND shaping clusters apart — are independently falsified in this repo's own artifacts dated the same day.
critiques.will_it_forget: Structural anti-forgetting (parameter-disjoint frozen experts + frozen substrate) is genuinely the strongest engineered part and would, in isolation, prevent KNOB overwrite — that claim is true and is real. But it is solving the wrong object. Forgetting in this system is downstream of ROUTING: a perfectly non-overwritable expert you cannot select at test is worthless. The router routes via nearest-frozen-prototype in the frozen context space, and that space is at chance-vs-control on CIFAR (above). Under a smeared/mis-routing softmax the mixed knob sum_j w_j{b_j,g_j} averages basins -> phase lands between attractors -> regression toward the naive ~0.13/0.20 collapse. Secondary: the "frozen substrate generalizes to tasks 2..5" assumption is unverified and the proposal's own main_risk admits it; J,P,g_shared are optimized on task 0 ONLY, so prototypes for later tasks can land in degenerate/overlapping regions. So forgetting of the head is prevented but forgetting/instability of ROUTING — the actual STEP-10 failure (final 0.256, forgetting 0.672) — is not addressed.
critiques.label_free_ok: Mechanically C2-legal (input-only drives c -> w at test; current-task labels used only for teacher-forcing the router during training, which is class-IL legal). The problem is not legality, it is that the label-free signal is empirically absent on CIFAR. step4a_readout.json (n=5/arm): MLP task-decode of L1 phase R6 mlp_cv ~0.294 vs R6s ~0.297 (identical), only +0.078 over the 0.236 shuffle floor; linear cv R6 0.255 vs R6s 0.251. The router reads exactly this descriptor. CPA-Lock's NO-GO gate demands slot-acc>=0.45; the measured ceiling is ~0.29 AND R6s decodes equally well, so even a hypothetical pass would fail C4. Label-free routing has real signal on shapes (where it is also coupling-agnostic) and ~no usable, synchrony-specific signal on CIFAR.
critiques.bug_risk: (1) Substrate/measurement category error: P and the pool are permutation/site-invariant mean-pool over sites (m2_primitives._pool_phase_state averages the C//n group axis); the "tighter clusters per bias-anchor b_t" can be renormalized away by c_norm GroupNorm / sphere_normalize since b_t is injected pre-norm at ladder.py:109 (c=self.c_norm(c)) — b_t added pre-norm gets normalized out, making the anchor a near no-op. (2) rank-4 dJ_t / b_t over a frozen J0 that dominates a T=3 iterated Kuramoto fixed point may be too weak to move the basin at all. (3) Teacher-forcing/soft-mix train-test mismatch: knobs trained at one-hot operating point, evaluated under smeared w through nonlinear settle — silent accuracy killer not caught by any joint upper bound. (4) Instance leak: router trained on current-task inputs over an instance-rich descriptor (instance_lift ~ task_lift on CIFAR) can route on exemplar identity, inflating train slot-acc while failing held-out within-shuffle. (5) The "cheapest milestone" reuses the joint pipeline but the headline number it expects (0.45-0.49 slot-acc) is a shapes number; on CIFAR the same probe will return ~chance and the bet is already exhausted.
critiques.cheapest_kill_test: Already essentially run — do NOT build any grow/freeze/expert machinery. The one load-bearing claim is "task-separable, synchrony-specific signal survives freezing into a routable form on CIFAR." Test it for ~1 GPU-hr: joint-train task 0, FREEZE {J,P,g}, compute per-task prototypes p_t = mean co-trained context on CIFAR, then measure held-out nearest-prototype slot-accuracy (chance 0.20) for BOTH R6 and R6s. Pre-registered GO only if slot-acc>=0.45 AND R6 beats R6s by a pre-set margin (e.g. >=0.10) paired. Prediction from existing artifacts (step5: R6 task_lift 0.16 == R6s 0.12, acc_within R6s>R6; step4a R6 mlp_cv 0.294==R6s 0.297): slot-acc lands ~0.25-0.30 and R6 ties R6s -> NO-GO on both the routability AND the C4/synchrony claims. This single probe kills CPA-Lock before any expert plumbing is written.
critiques.single_best_fix: Stop importing the shapes 0.49 number; re-run the kill test ABOVE on CIFAR with R6-minus-R6s gap (not R6 absolute) as the pre-registered headline. When it fails (it will, per step4a/step5), accept the diagnosis the repo already reached: the phase-context task-channel ceiling on natural single-object/global-pooled inputs is ARCHITECTURAL (confirmed by step4b null on the readout side AND step4b/contrastive null on the dynamics side), not an anchoring/freezing problem. The only scientifically new lever the artifacts leave open is a CONSTRUCT REDESIGN: replace the rotation/permutation-invariant mean-pool descriptor + single-object construct with a genuine multi-object/spatial-binding task where AKOrN's coupling has distinct spatial groups to bind (research-log.md flags construct-mismatch as the root cause: weak on the engineered binding task, strong only on real CIFAR's many-pair structure). CPA-Lock as written spends its entire budget structurally protecting a channel this repo has already measured to be non-separable and coupling-agnostic on CIFAR.

critiques.will_it_actually_fix_drift: Mostly no — it relabels the problem behind two false premises about the actual code. (1) The "band-resolved pool (per-ω-band coherence, not the S_N-invariant mean-pool)" is the load-bearing element that exposes separation to g, but it does not exist and contradicts the real descriptor. m2_primitives.py:_pool_phase_state (248-266) + h3.py:group_directions (490-515) reshape (B,C,H,W)->(B,C//n,n,H,W)->(n_sites,n) and then AVERAGE over all sites (mean, meansq over axis 0) into a fixed 2n-dim vector (n=4 -> 8-dim; context_dim_for_n line 89). Any per-band/per-frequency structure lives on the site axis that is averaged away — this is the identical, campaign-DECISIVE PSA flaw ("slots are invisible to g"). FROST needs a wholesale per-band pool rewrite it never specifies, and which discards the permutation/site-count invariance the descriptor was built for. (2) "AKOrN already has per-oscillator learnable omega" is false for this config: ladder.py:29 sets global_omg=True (single shared frequency, init_omg=1.0). Menara's distinct-frequency-clusters argument needs per-cluster omega; you cannot get distinct ω-bands by tuning one global scalar without re-architecting omega to be per-band, which is unbuilt. (3) Even granting both fixes, Menara stability of distinct-ω clusters is an asymptotic property of the autonomous Kuramoto ODE; the project's context is a no_grad numpy snapshot of L1 state after T=3 inner steps from a fixed init, pooled — it is not the asymptotic synchronization manifold, so the theorem's stability guarantee does not transfer to the quantity g actually reads. The drift claim ("basin's existence does not depend on the part that changes") is asserted at the ODE level but the realized context depends on the SHARED J, shared global omega, and shared GroupNorm scaffolding through an iterated fixed point — exactly the cross-task coupling the campaign flagged (PSA flaw 3): B's training can move the basin A's input converges to even with A's own params frozen.
critiques.will_it_forget: The grow-and-freeze of per-task params (ω_t, g_t requires_grad_(False)) genuinely isolates the DYNAMICS' per-task pieces — the strongest and only real claim, shared with PALM. But it does not fix the actual M3 forgetting, which the harness localizes elsewhere: g is SHARED and is the thing that collapsed to ~0.13 (m3_R6_probe.log). FROST relocates forgetting into ROUTING error by its own admission (main_risk). The label-free band-selector itself has no anti-forgetting: adding band t+1 changes the global argmax-coherence landscape, and there is no replay of routing decisions for old tasks, so old inputs can mis-route even with perfectly retained heads ("separable jointly, forgets sequentially" at the router) — the precise failure the campaign named for PALM. The CoRe null-space penalty is applied to a no_grad/numpy converged-phase output that currently has no gradient path, so it is not implementable as described without the unbuilt differentiable port.
critiques.label_free_ok: Formally C2-clean at test (argmax band-coherence uses input only; task id used only in class-IL training). But the substantive label-free signal is ALREADY FALSIFIED in this harness, which is fatal: the morning report and step4a show the rotation/permutation-invariant descriptor decodes task at R6 0.186 vs R6s 0.104 and is R6==R6s for the selector (+0.078 over floor) — coupling-agnostic, so any selector "success" fails the must-beat-R6s bar. Worse, ~54% of the context lift is INSTANCE-level not task-level (within-task-shuffle decomposition, n=20), so a coherence selector trained on current-task inputs can route on exemplar identity (an instance leak) and inflate train-time band-selector accuracy while genuine task routing stays at chance. FROST's claim that ω-banding "makes the dead selector loaded" is the untested crux and rests on the unbuilt per-band pool + per-band omega.
critiques.bug_risk: High and silent. (1) THE HEADLINE SILENT BUG: implementing FROST on the existing pool (the cheapest path) produces the SAME 2n-dim averaged context for every band — band-local coherence collapses to the global descriptor, argmax-coherence routes on nothing, and the code runs green while testing nothing (identical to the campaign's PSA "runs green, tests nothing" warning). (2) global_omg=True: a per-band ω offset injected expecting per-oscillator omega will broadcast/alias incorrectly or be a no-op; shared omega couples all bands, contradicting frequency isolation.
