(3) requires_grad_(False) does not stop Adam from stepping frozen tensors if momentum buffers persist / grads flow via the single shared J or g weight tensor — must mask the GRADIENT every step and reset optimizer state, not just set requires_grad. (4) The whole context path is torch.no_grad()+numpy (_phase_context lines 72-75); "co-train pool/J with grad flow" and the CoRe null-space penalty on converged phase silently have zero gradient unless a differentiable port (the nonexistent palr_pool.py the prompt references) is written and unit-tested to 1e-4 WITH grad. (5) Menara stability invoked on a T=3, fixed-init, pooled snapshot — not the asymptotic manifold — so the theoretical guarantee is mis-applied.
critiques.cheapest_kill_test: Before writing any FROST machinery, run the falsifier the campaign already has scaffolding for: on a 2-task slice, build the band-LOCAL coherence descriptor FROST relies on, and probe (a) task-decode accuracy of band-local coherence on held-out task-0 vs task-1 inputs for R6 vs R6s, and (b) within-task-SHUFFLE the labels to strip instance leakage. KILL if band-coherence decode is R6==R6s OR collapses to chance under within-task-shuffle (it will, because the pool averages over the band axis and omega is global) — i.e. the frequency-separation premise does not survive the same R6-vs-R6s + shuffle firewalls that already killed the global descriptor. This is <1 GPU-hr and reuses h3.py decode + the n=20 within-shuffle decomposition. Equally cheap structural kill: assert that group_directions/_pool_phase_state expose a per-band axis to g — they do not (it is averaged), so the design is code-contradicted before any training.
critiques.single_best_fix: Stop attacking separability and attack the proven ceiling: the FROZEN-RANDOM 64-dim TRUNK, not context drift, is what caps everything — the ORACLE one-hot context (perfectly separable, drift-free by construction) reaches only 0.524 in this exact harness (m3_oracle_a/b.log). No amount of ω-band separability can exceed an oracle that is already far below "solved," and FROST's 0.40-0.50 target sits AT/BELOW that oracle. If pursued at all, the one change that makes FROST actually test its thesis is to first BUILD AND UNIT-TEST (grad to 1e-4) a genuinely per-band differentiable pool that does NOT average over the band/site axis, make omega per-band (drop global_omg), and verify on the 2-task slice that band-local coherence beats R6s AND a centroid-distance baseline AND survives within-task-shuffle — and accept the negative the moment it ties R6s, which prior evidence says it will.

synthesis.ranked_menu:
RANKED (best expected value per GPU-hr, given the verified artifacts):

#1 PHLOX (freeze-and-grow + energy-margin selector) — VERDICT: pursue, but in the critic's amended form. Pitch: freeze the shared substrate {J, pool, c_norm} after task 0, grow a parameter-disjoint Kuramoto bias-anchor + generator column per task, route label-free at test. KEY RISK: its named selector (argmin pooled AKOrN energy es=-sim) is the EXACT quantity measured dead in step4a (R6 mlp_cv 0.29 == R6s 0.30, +0.08 over floor) — so it ships stable-but-mis-routing at the same ~0.20-0.27 ceiling. The freeze-and-grow skeleton is the only genuinely sound, drift-killing idea across all five; the selector is the liability.

#2 CPA-Lock (frozen co-trained context + prototype routing) — VERDICT: borderline; only its kill-test is worth running, and it doubles as PHLOX's. Pitch: freeze the co-trained context space, route by nearest frozen per-task prototype over disjoint experts. KEY RISK: its entire bet rests on "co-trained context ~0.49 on CIFAR," which I confirmed is the SHAPES oracle (step4c acc_real ~0.99), NOT CIFAR. On CIFAR the routable signal is task_lift ~0.16, coupling-agnostic. Freezing a non-separable space just locks in the overlap. Centroid/prototype routing is, however, the correct replacement for PHLOX's dead energy selector — so CPA-Lock's routing idea should be folded into PHLOX rather than run standalone.

#3 PhaseClust-CoRe (Menara Omega clusters + CoRe low-rank + centroid distillation) — VERDICT: likely dead-end as stated; over-built. Pitch: per-task learnable natural-frequency offsets to make Menara-stable phase clusters, low-rank context-space interventions, centroid distillation. KEY RISK: its own M0 gate (task_lift >= 0.45, R6-R6s >= +0.07 significant) ALREADY FAILS on existing step5 data (0.16, +0.043, p~0.12). Omega is an R5 mechanism (synchrony-OFF arm) so a random-coupling R6s can satisfy the same cluster-stability condition — the claimed "R6 must beat R6s" reason is structurally broken. Three redundant anti-forgetting guards solve a problem (knob overwrite) that freeze-and-grow already solves with one.

#4 FROST (omega-banded sub-tori, frequency-multiplexed tasks) — VERDICT: doomed as written. Pitch: give each task its own frozen natural-frequency band so tasks separate in frequency (orthogonal to image content) and are Menara-drift-immune. KEY RISK: TWO false premises about the actual code — (a) omega is global_omg=True (single shared scalar in ladder.py:29), not per-oscillator, so per-band omega is unbuilt; (b) the band-resolved pool it needs does not exist — the real descriptor averages over the site axis (_pool_phase_state), so band structure is averaged away and the selector reads nothing while running green. Menara stability is asymptotic-ODE; the context is a T=3 fixed-init numpy snapshot, so the theorem does not transfer. Most novel core, least implementable, highest silent-bug risk.

#5 PhaseVSA-WTA (sparse-block-code quantization of context) — VERDICT: doomed; quantization-of-noise fallacy. Pitch: hard winner-take-all over frozen-random codebooks turns the noisy continuous phase context into a near-orthogonal discrete code with drift dead-zones. KEY RISK: hard WTA cannot create I(task;context) the continuous descriptor lacks; Frady/Clarkson orthogonality only holds for DISTINCT codewords, and step4a shows the clouds already overlap (only +0.08 over floor, coupling-agnostic). A dead-zone wide enough to kill drift is wide enough to merge tasks. STE through hard WTA invites silent code-collapse; the proposed unit tests (grad-finite, dead-zone-invariant) pass while the code dies. d_ctx=16 split M ways destroys the 1/sqrt(d) orthogonality guarantee.
synthesis.top_pick: PHLOX — but ONLY the freeze-and-grow skeleton, with the energy-argmin selector REPLACED by centroid/prototype routing (the single best fix the PHLOX critic and CPA-Lock both converge on). Treat energy-margin as an ablation arm, never the load-bearing router. Concretely: PHLOX-skeleton + CPA-Lock-routing is the strongest single bet on the table.
synthesis.top_pick_why: It is the only proposal that genuinely fixes the DIAGNOSED drift (structural parameter isolation: freeze {J, pool, c_norm} after task 0, requires_grad_(False) on old anchors/heads — task B's optimizer physically cannot move task A's basin). I verified the drift problem is real and severe (step10 R6 forget 0.672) and that freeze-and-grow is the one mechanism all five proposals share for it. Implementability in the harness is the best of the five: it does NOT require the missing per-band omega (FROST) or a dimensionally-implausible SBC (VSA); the differentiable pool it needs exists (/private/tmp/palr_pool.py, unit-tested) and "only" needs wiring into m2_hypernet (which currently pools under no_grad+numpy). Bug-risk is moderate and known: the c_norm GroupNorm at ladder.py:109 will partly renormalize a pre-norm additive anchor (inject post-norm or as a multiplicative gain), and Adam will step frozen params unless removed from the param-group. Crucially, swapping the dead energy selector for prototype routing removes its single fatal flaw at zero extra cost — and even in the worst case it ships a clean grow-and-freeze prototype-routing method PLUS a measured negative on whether AKOrN energy beats a centroid baseline. That is a publishable outcome either way.
synthesis.first_milestone: CHEAPEST FIRST MILESTONE (do NOT build the full method first — run an OFFLINE readout test on artifacts that already exist, ~30-60 min, no training): Take the ALREADY-TRAINED R6 and R6s step5 CIFAR backbones. For a 2-task CIFAR-10 slice, compute three label-free routers over held-out, WITHIN-TASK-SHUFFLED inputs (shuffle strips the ~0.21 instance-leak so you measure TASK routing, not exemplar identity): (A) PHLOX's own energy-argmin (pooled es=-sim under an injected per-task anchor); (B) nearest-stored-prototype/centroid in the co-trained context space (the proposed fix); (C) centroid-L2 baseline = Centroid-Distance-Distillation. Score slot-accuracy (chance 0.50) for R6 vs R6s on all three. This reuses h3 decode + the existing within-shuffle decomposition and needs zero new training. It directly tests the ONE load-bearing claim (is there a label-free, synchrony-specific, task-separable signal that survives freezing on CIFAR?) before any grow/freeze/anchor machinery is written.
synthesis.go_no_go: GO requires ALL THREE jointly on within-task-shuffled held-out CIFAR: (1) best router slot-acc >= 0.75 (chance 0.50); (2) R6 beats R6s by a clear paired margin (pre-register R6-minus-R6s as the HEADLINE, not R6 absolute — the gap is the synchrony claim); (3) the winning router beats the centroid-L2 baseline (otherwise the AKOrN coupling is decorative and you have a progressive-net, not a synchrony result). NO-GO / accept the CIFAR negative if: energy-argmin ties or loses to centroid (then drop energy, keep prototype routing as an honest method + measured null on energy), OR R6 ties R6s (synchrony is coupling-agnostic on natural images — the thesis fails on CIFAR even if accuracy rises), OR all routers sit near chance+0.08 (the step4a-measured ceiling reproduces — phase task-capacity is architectural and you write the characterized-limit negative). Prediction from verified artifacts: energy-argmin likely ties centroid or R6s; prototype routing is the only arm with a chance of clearing the bar, and even it starts from a weak task_lift ~0.16 base.
synthesis.likely_deadends: FROST (#4) and PhaseVSA-WTA (#5) are likely dead-ends and I would not spend GPU on either: FROST is blocked by two code-contradicted premises (global_omg=True scalar omega; the pool averages away the band axis) and mis-applies an asymptotic-ODE theorem to a T=3 numpy snapshot; VSA commits a quantization-of-noise fallacy (WTA cannot manufacture task information that step4a shows is absent, and d_ctx=16 breaks the near-orthogonality it depends on). PhaseClust-CoRe (#3) is a softer dead-end: its own go-gate already fails on existing data and its Omega lever is an R5 (synchrony-off) mechanism, so it cannot deliver a coupling-specific win. Two SPECIFIC SUB-IDEAS are also dead regardless of wrapper and should be banned from any go-gate: (a) the energy-argmin / pooled-es selector (step4a: R6==R6s, +0.08 over floor); (b) any contrastive/margin separation loss on live phase (step4b: NULL, p~0.84) — PHLOX's L_margin is this renamed, so route on prototypes and demote energy/margin to ablation. Also dead: any claim resting on "0.49-0.52 on CIFAR" — that number is SHAPES; the CIFAR oracle is unmeasured.
synthesis.honest_outlook: Honestly low — I'd put ~15-25% on PHLOX-skeleton+prototype-routing rescuing CIFAR to a clean, synchrony-specific, publishable result, and that is the most optimistic of the five. The repo's OWN diagnosis (MORNING-REPORT) already calls the phase task-channel ceiling "architectural, not training-effort," and I verified every supporting number: weak task_lift (0.16), coupling-agnostic gap (+0.043, n.s.), dead selector substrate (+0.08 over floor), online collapse with R6s forgetting LESS than R6, and NO measured CIFAR oracle headroom at all. Freeze-and-grow is real and WILL stop the drift — but it converts drift-forgetting into routing-error forgetting, and routing reads a channel measured near-dead on natural images. The most probable outcome is a clean, well-controlled NEGATIVE: "structural isolation eliminates oscillator self-forgetting, but the AKOrN phase context is not task-separable on natural images, so label-free routing caps accuracy — an architectural limit of global-pooled single-object phase descriptors." That is a trustworthy, oracle-anchored result worth shipping. The ONE thing that could change the odds materially is NOT on this menu: the construct-mismatch lever the critics independently converge on — first MEASURE the CIFAR oracle ceiling (one-hot context through the frozen trunk, never run) to confirm headroom exists at all, and if the trunk caps it, widen the frozen-random trunk (C1-legal: capacity, not task-adaptivity) and/or move to a genuine multi-object/spatial-binding construct where AKOrN coupling actually has groups to bind. I'd raise that with the PI as the real fork before committing to any of the five.
